Who I Am

A little About Me

Hi! I'm Azra, an almost-graduate Electronic and Computer Engineering student based in Wrocław, Poland. I love building things at the edge of hardware and software, the layer where code makes physical stuff actually happen.

From training CNNs to read traffic signs, to wiring up a Bluetooth robot, to designing PCBs in Altium. I'm most comfortable when I'm figuring something out the hard way. I believe the best engineers are the ones who aren't afraid to break things.

I'm bilingual, I think in Turkish and debug in English. Currently looking for internships and interesting embedded or AI projects to sink my teeth into.

🔬

More oscilloscope hours than I'd like to admit — but every waveform tells a story.

🤖

My robot did crash into a wall during demo. We called it "boundary testing."

🚦

Taught a CNN to read traffic signs. It's about 85% confident. Same as me.

⚡

A well-placed LED blink test solves 50% of embedded problems. Proven fact.
